title: Option to ignore invalid tag lists from a registry?
body:
With the [exception of digests](https://github.com/mtrmac/image/blob/7d50f7436dc08342c58cdbd04306acf41daa0f2b/docker/docker_image.go#L94-L101) invalid tag formats result in an error. As a result, when a repository managed by another entity adds an invalid tag, there is no recourse for listing valid tags. This is currently the case with [datadog/agent](https://gallery.ecr.aws/datadog/agent), which has the likely erroneous tags `-ot-beta-jmx` and `-ot-beta`.

It would be preferred to optionally ignore invalid tags instead of erroring.

comments.body:
Thanks for your report.

I don’t know about adding an _option_ for that throughout the stack, maybe we should just warn. Or fail + still produce results, and let some consumers who value that kind of output still use the results.

OTOH if we know the server is not following the protocol, it’s a bit risky to continue issuing “next page” requests.

Anyway, this needs to be solved in c/image first, so transferring there.
